titlestmt.titleproper: Finding aid for the Wanda Coleman papers LSC.2282
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kim Calder, June 16, 2016; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé. Finding aid was updated by Chloe Cipolla under supervision from Kelly Besser, 2025.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-06 13:59:04 -0800.

physdesc.extent: (231 document boxes, 2 half-document boxes, 3 record cartons, 5 flat boxes, and 5 shoeboxes)

did.abstract: Wanda Coleman was born on November 13, 1946 and grew up in the Watts neighborhood of Los Angeles, CA. Coleman's poetry is widely anthologized and published, and her poetry collection Bathwater Wine received the 1999 Lenore Marshall Poetry Prize. In addition to her poetic career, Coleman was also successful across genres. She had a well-known column in the Los Angeles Times and won an Emmy for her writing on Days of Our Lives. The collection contains work produced by Coleman between 1960 and 2013, including manuscripts and drafts of her published poetry and prose, journalism, writing for film and television, and spoken word recordings, as well as many unpublished writings. In addition, there is a wealth of correspondence with major literary figures and institutions, activist materials, and ephemera relating to Coleman's professional and personal life.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.
did.langmaterial: Materials are primarily in English, some materials in Dutch and Italian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED DIGITAL/AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Digital/audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Wanda Coleman papers (Collection 2282). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by Kim Calder under the supervision of Jillian Cuellar and Courtney Dean in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), 2015-2016.

Additional processing of 2017 acquisition materials by CFPRT scholar Chloe Cipolla with assistance from CFPRT scholar Laila Jaffer and archivist Kelly Besser, 2025. Nicole Alvarado of UCLA Library Preservation and Conservation Department provided expert care on the housing and cleaning of the rolodex (Box 186) and typewriter (Box 247). Monographs cataloged by Josh Fiala.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's Library Collections and Archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wanda Coleman was born on November 13, 1946 in Watts and raised in South Central Los Angeles. When she was eighteen, she married her first husband, Jerry Coleman, with whom she had two children, Anthony and Luanda Tunisia. Coleman retained custody of the children when she and Jerry divorced in 1969, and struggled to survive and write as a single mother. She worked as an editor for Players, a conscious Black gentleman's magazine, from 1972-1974, and in the mid 1970s, she moved to Hollywood, where she became an active participant in the spoken word and poetry communities, penning works for Studio Watts and becoming a fixture at Beyond Baroque in Venice. In 1977, she published a chapbook, Art in the Court of the Blue Fag, followed by a full-length collection of poems, Mad Dog Black Lady (1979) with Black Sparrow Press, beginning a publishing relationship that would last for over thirty years and produce twelve books in various genres. Her third child, Ian, with her second husband Stephen Grant, was born in 1979. Coleman was also an acclaimed performance artist known for her impactful readings. In the 1980s, her presence in the LA spoken word scene led her to collaborate with seminal punk figures such as Exene Cervenka (X) and Lydia Lunch, and she worked with New Alliance records to release a number of solo and split recordings. Coleman attended Valley Junior College and Cal State L.A., and went on to teach at UCLA extension, Cal State Long Beach, Naropa, and Loyola Marymount University, where she held the Fletcher Jones chair in literature and writing. Prolific across genres, Coleman wrote poetry, short stories, novels, nonfiction, and plays, as well as scripts for film and television, winning an Emmy for her work on on Days of Our Lives in 1976 and working as a featured columnist for the Los Angeles Times. Coleman is one of the most widely anthologized and published poets of her generation, appearing in prestigious collections such as Postmodern American Poetry: A Norton Anthology and The Oxford Anthology of African-American Poetry. Her poetry collection Bathwater Wine received the 1999 Lenore Marshall Poetry Prize, Mercurochrome: New Poems (2001) was a finalist for the National Book Award, and her honors include a Guggenheim, an NEA fellowship, and the Poetry Society of America's Shelley Memorial Award. With her husband of thirty years, poet and visual artist Austin Straus, she hosted "The Poetry Connexion" on KPFK from 1981-1994. She died November 22, 2013. In November of 2015, the Ascot branch of the Los Angeles public library in Watts, where Coleman spent many of her formative years reading and writing, was renamed the Wanda Coleman Branch in her honor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection chronicles Coleman's entire career (1960-2013), and includes many materials that provide insight into her process, such as meticulously kept records of submissions, publications, and rejections; multiple drafts and revisions of poems, stories, and articles with Coleman's edits; correspondence with John Martin at Black Sparrow Press regarding major works; a wealth of correspondence with major literary figures such as E. Ethelbert Miller and Sesshu Foster; many activist materials; flyers and other ephemera related to Coleman's public performances and other "gigs"; teaching materials; photographs; recordings; and many unpublished writings including a novel, a collection of newer short stories, and screenplays.

list.item: Series 1: Correspondence, 1960-2013

Series 2: Works by Wanda Coleman, 1961-2012
Subseries 2.1: Poetry, 1968-2010
Subseries 2.2: Fiction, 1975-2012
Subseries 2.3: Nonfiction, 1973-2012
Subseries 2.4: Spoken Word, TV, Film, and Theater, 1970-2011
Subseries 2.5: Juvenilia and Visual Art, 1961-1998
Subseries 2.6: As Editor, 1980-2009
Series 3: Gigs/PR, 1969-2013
Series 4: Subject Files, 1963-2012

Series 5: Memorabilia, 1963-2016
Subseries 5.1: Library, 1964-2014
Subseries 5.2: Personal Works, Items, and Ephemera, 1961-2013
Subseries 5.3: Posthumous, 2013-2016

p.extref: Black Sparrow Press records, UC Berkeley Bancroft Library (Collection BANC MSS 97/40 c).
S.A. Griffin collection of underground poetry, Scott Wannberg, and the Carma Bums, circa 1950-2015, UCLA Special Collections (Collection 2248).
Watts Writers' Workshop stories, lesson books, and other material (Collection 0177).

altformavail.head: Digital Images of Box 184 Available
altformavail.p: Digital reproductions of materials in Box 184 are available for viewing in the Library Special Collections reading room.

controlaccess.subject: African American women poets -- 20th century -- Archives
African American women authors -- 20th century -- Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Contains personal and professional letters and emails to and from Coleman, as well as correspondence and materials detailing her submissions process and rejections/acceptances. Includes letters to and from John Martin, her publisher at Black Sparrow Press, and correspondence with major figures such as Amiri Baraka, Sherman Alexie, Octavia Butler, Exene Cervenka, Cornelius Eady, Sesshu Foster, S.A. Griffin, Allen Ginsberg, C.S. Giscombe, Anna Halprin (San Francisco Dancers' Workshop), Terrance Hayes, bell hooks, Yusef Komunyakaa, Audre Lorde, Clarence Major, E. Ethelbert Miller, Ishmael Reed, Diane Wakoski, and Anne Waldman.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series maintains original order. Coleman organized the bulk of her personal and professional correspondence alphabetically, but the latter part of the series includes correspondence not included within this alphabetical arrangement, ordered chronologically and by type. Correspondence with major individuals and institutions has been gathered throughout processing and is included within the alphabetical sequence.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.
SOME ITEMS IN THIS SERIES HAVE BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to John Martin (Black Sparrow Press) regarding Will Alexander and correspondence with Jeffrey Renard Allen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from Coleman regarding her controversial 2002 review of Angelou's A Song Flew Up to Heaven, along with drafts of her essay on the public's response, "Black on Black: Fear and Reviewing in Los Angeles."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman did all the secretarial work for her brother George Evans' A.R.T. workshop for high schoolers. She used the pseudonym "Joyce Grant," (a combination of her middle name and her second husband's last name).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Laurel Ann Bogen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Malcolm Boyd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts of and correspondence regarding Brown's 2002 interview with Coleman, published in Callaloo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Jericho Brown and Fred Burkhart and emails regarding Charles Bukowski.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence related to the California Arts Council.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with and ephemera relating to Exene Cervenka (X).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Marilyn Chin.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: City of Los Angeles Artist Fellowship

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman's application to and correspondence regarding the City of Los Angeles Artists' Fellowships (she was the first writer to be awarded one).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Bill Clinton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence relating to Jayne Cortez memorial panel.

scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Jim Cushing (Dr. Punk).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to Kwame Dawes and correspondence with Fred Dewey (Beyond Baroque).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with family members (Evans).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Susannah Foster.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with David R. Godine, the publisher who took over Black Sparrow titles after the press shut down.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Marilyn Hacker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Odie Hawkins, Eloise Klein Healy, and Lee Hickman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Jack Hirschman regarding a reading he and Coleman gave at Beyond Baroque, along with Wanda's set list of poems and event flyers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence to and from Austin Strauss, Wanda's third husband. Includes condolence letters sent to Straus after Coleman's passing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Alan Snitow regarding his documentary Blacks and Jews, which Coleman participated in the making of.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to Gerald Stern.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence related to the Hollywood/TV writer age discrimination class action lawsuit Coleman joined, represented by law firm Sprenger and Lane.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Bobbi Sykes and Amber Tamblyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Stanley's entry on Coleman for the Dictionary of Literary Biography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Amber Tamblyn and correspondence regarding the 1999 World Heavyweight Championship Poetry Bout in Taos (Coleman vs. Sherman Alexie).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lisa Teasley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Includes many originals of early poems and short stories sent to magazines such as Essence and Poetry, and associated correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes many originals of early poems and short stories.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes many originals of early poems and short stories.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes additional correspondence from the 2017 library acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a log of poems submitted to and accepted by journals, as well as some notes by Coleman regarding her experiences at readings during this time period.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and information relating to Wanda's succesful 1984 fellowship application.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In addition to correspondence regarding Wanda's fellowship and subsequent applications, this folder also contains letters of reference by Wanda for other authors' applications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to a Guggenheim Fellowship application Coleman submitted on behalf of her brother, George Evans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed emails sent and received by Coleman; additions from the 2017 library acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence from the 2017 library acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence written by Coleman from the 2017 library acquisition. Includes a partial 1998 letter written by Coleman to her daughter Tunisia, husband Austin, and publisher John Martin that appears to serve as her will.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence from the 2017 library acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence from the 2017 library acquisition.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: File contains 4 compact discs. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books and manuscripts from other authors sent to Coleman, often with additional correspondence attached. Works by Jeff Allen, Rae Armantrout, Jan Beatty, Paul Beatty (includes blurb by Coleman), Elaine Brown, Soleyl Dahi/Lawrence Ferlinghetti, George Herms, Ellen Maybe, Sesshu Foster, Gloria Oden, Akilah Oliver (annotated by Coleman), Sylvia Rosen, Matthew Shenoda, Amber Tamblyn, Debra Weinstein. Includes PhD dissertation by Priscilla Ann Brown on Coleman's poetry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Many drafts of published and unpublished works by Wanda Coleman: poetry, fiction, nonfiction, spoken word, film, television and theater writings, and juvenilia, as well as volumes edited by Coleman. Includes related correspondence and digital materials. Highlights include correspondence with John Martin at Black Sparrow Press regarding her publications there, many unproduced screenplays, and unpublished collections of poetry and short stories.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is broken into subseries by genre, and each is ordered chronologically. Within subseries 2.1-2.3 (poetry, fiction, nonfiction), published manuscripts lead the series, followed by unpublished manuscripts, then individual drafts.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.
SOME ITEMS IN THIS SERIES HAVE BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Published and unpublished collections of Coleman's poetry, as well as drafts of individual poems. Drafts are organized chronologically and include both published and unpublished poems with Coleman's edits. Includes some related correspondence. Materials for Coleman's collection of poems Ostinato Vamps (2003) are not included.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SOME ITEMS IN THIS SUBSERIES HAVE BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.p: List of poems and instances of their publication.

scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with John Martin.

scopecontent.p: Materials relating to the book's publication, including photos from the publication party, correspondence, and reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This manuscript, which eventually became Bathwater Wine, is titled God Bless The Fire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Pudding House Press, the chapbook's publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Love Project is a collaboration between Coleman and her husband, Austin Straus. Includes correspondence with the publisher, Red Hen Press, and drawings from the book (some originals and some copies).

scopecontent.p: Manuscript includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Two early unpublished manuscripts titled Midnight Carries Me South Along Illusion Avenue and My Anger, My Prison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early unpublished manuscripts titled The Laying Down Blues and Other Laments: black blue street verse and A Gun Full of Bullets.

did.unittitle: "On Image and Self Creation: A Meditation"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of and correspondence relating to "Bathsheba's Psalm," an unpublished translation intended for an anthology of Anglo-Saxon poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Published and unpublished novels and short story collections, as well as individual drafts of stories. Includes related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Mambo Hips and Make Believe (Fourzon materials)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Coleman drew on the biography of and her correspondence with Pamela Fourzon in writing Mambo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder, titled "Mambo Hips and Make Believe 1999," contains materials related to the novel as well as additional unrelated papers, mainly correspondence between Coleman and publisher John Martin.

did.unittitle: The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (second rough)
The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (third rough)
The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (work manuscript 1985)
The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (work manuscript 1985, copy 2)
The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (second rewrite)
The Saturday Night's Over and Charlie Says He Loves Me, Blues (manuscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished volume of collected late stories.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collaboration with husband Austin Strauss. Folder contains manuscript and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include: "Fairlane Phantasms," "Howlin' with the Woofdog," "The Authentic Cannibal and Friend," "The Emancipation of Imamu Afif," "Wife in Abstract," "Being Davy's Sister," and "The Oroville Experience."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Published essay collections, as well as individual essays, articles, and book reviews, such as Coleman's controversial review of Maya Angelou's A Song Flung Up to Heaven. There is a large quantity of material from her time as a columnist for Los Angeles Times Magazine. Includes related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Native in a Strange Land (book proposal, rough)
Native in a Strange Land (book proposal)
Native in a Strange Land (original manuscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Bound volume contains some related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Native in a Strange Land (work rough)
Native in a Strange Land (first proofs, author's)
Native in a Strange Land (first proofs, copy 1)
Native in a Strange Land (first proofs, copy 3)
Native in a Strange Land (second proofs)
Native in a Strange Land (second proofs, copy 2)

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS ITEM HAS BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and correspondence relating to Coleman's controversial review of Maya Angelou's A Song Flung Up to Heaven in the Los Angeles Times Book Review. Also includes Coleman's annotated copy of Song and other books by Angelou.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials from a binder labeled "memoirs."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings related to pornography and erotica, written for Wyngate and Bevins Publications, publisher of gentlemen's magazines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes annotated draft and research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and writings gathered by Coleman for the purpose of writing her memoirs.

did.unittitle: "Poetry, Race, and the Power to Change The World"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "9/11 Blues", "Striving to Be a Man: Gender-Altering Forces in Post-Feminist America", "Re-Affirmative Actions" (address for the 22nd Annual African American Awards Ceremony), and "How To Cure Generic Language" (creative writing workshop speech, 2001).

did.unittitle: Spoken Word, TV, Film, and Theater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Contains recorded material from New Alliance records including Coleman's projects with Exene Cervenka, Lydia Lunch, and Michelle T. Clinton; episodes written for popular television shows such as Days of our Lives (for which she won an Emmy), Starsky and Hutch, and Sanford and Son; multiple unproduced screenplays, including Rimbaud's Demon, co-written with Murray Wolfe; and plays, including The Product, written in collaboration with Jim Woods of Studio Watts. Includes related correspondence.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS SUBSERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to Coleman's spoken word projects with Exene Cervenka, Michelle T. Clinton, and Lydia Lunch; compilations "Black and Tan Club" and "Jazzspeak"; as well as solo recordings "Black Angeles," "Twin Sisters," "Black and Blue News," "Beserk on Hollywood Blvd." and "High Priestess of Word." Includes digital materials.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Files contain nine 1/8 inch cassettes and one compact disc. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: File contains one 1/8 inch audio cassette. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

unittitle.title: Black Girl in Search of God

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Coleman's adaptation of George Bernard Shaw's play of the same name.

did.unittitle: Temperature's Rising and Blues Part of Town

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Play developed with Jim Woods, president of Studio Watts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Coleman won an Emmy for her writing on Days.

unittitle.title: Are You Listening To Me Sweetie?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes episodes co-written with Christopher Joy for Starsky and Hutch and Sanford and Son.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Coleman developed this story as both a screenplay and a novel under these two titles. Box 200 contains manuscript drafts (as Independently Blue) with edits by Coleman and her husband Austin.

did.unittitle: Operation Move: The Trial of Ramona Africa (research)

unittitle.title: Operation Move: The Trial of Ramona Africa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Angel Street episode.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 149, Folder 1 contains research material consulted during writing process.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short play draft; includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Screenplay drafts of Beulah Land, a mini-series about a 19th century Southern plantation and materials/correspondence relating to the Coalition Against the Airing of Beulah Land.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman occasionally acted as editor for other poet's work, most significantly for her friend Susannah Foster, whose manuscript Coleman edited after Foster's death.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS SUBSERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman's edits of poet Patrick Bianucci's manuscript and correspondence regarding her efforts to get it published.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of three manuscripts of works by Susannah Foster that Coleman edited.

did.unittitle: Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (original manuscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Coleman's introduction to the volume of poetry by Susannah Foster she edited and worked to have published.

did.unittitle: Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (work manuscript 1)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Coleman's edits to Foster's manuscript.

did.unittitle: Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (work manuscript 2)
Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (second proofs)
Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (galley proofs)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One proof contains Coleman's edits. Also contains some loose correspondence.

did.unittitle: Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (Foster notebooks)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 202, Folder 1 contains photocopies of diaries and early writings by Susannah Foster (under her birth name Andrea Campbell Cox).

did.unittitle: Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (Coleman essay)
Earthbound in Betty Grable's Shoes (editor) (manuscript rejects)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Selected prose and short stories by Susannah Foster.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Alternate version of Coleman's edition of Susannah Foster's collected stories and prose.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Coleman edited and promoted this volume of Susannah Foster's stories and "miscellany."

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: File contains four compact discs. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: On behalf of the Multi-Ethnic Coalition of Los Angeles Artists, Coleman edited a special issue of High Performance magazine, "The Verdict and the Violence" (1992). A companion CD was also made. Includes digital media.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Box 115, folder 1 contains four microcassettes. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, ephemera such as programs, flyers, and photographs, and other documentation relating to "gigs" such as readings and lectures, serving as a panelist for various awards and grants, her time as editor of Players Magazine, and teaching jobs. She kept much of this material in "PR" files, and also included is her correspondence with the Guy Joyce PR agency, who she employed for a short time to represent her. Of particular interest are the files from her time at Loyola Marymount University as the Fletcher Jones chair in literature and writing and her correspondence with legal advisors after the university chose not to continue her appointment.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series is ordered chronologically. All teaching materials were kept together to maintain original order, and appear at the series' end.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SOME ITEMS IN THIS SERIES HAVE BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to Coleman's plays The Product, Black Girl in Search of God (George Bernard Shaw adaptation) produced by Studio Watts; Ceremony of Us (collaboration between Studio Watts and Anna Halprin of the San Francisco Dancers' Workshop, in which Coleman performed); ephemera, and correspondence.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS ITEM HAS BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to Coleman's job in the film industry, as both a screenwriter and script editor. Of note is correspondence from the Writers Guild of America related to Coleman's participation on the Committee of Black Writers, as well as a flip file of story ideas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes PR materials related to Mad Dog Black Lady. Box 150, Folder 6 includes photographs by George Evans of Coleman at the Whisky-a-Go-Go, where she performed in 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents relating to The Poetry Connexion, a radio show hosted by Coleman and Austin Straus on KPFK. Folder contains several transcripts of shows.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 217, Folder 16 contains materials related to Coleman's spoken word career.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes large poster from the festival.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS ITEM HAS BEEN DIGITIZED. Please contact the reference desk for details on arranging access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents have been removed from the original binder briefcase they were housed in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing material from the "Living Blues" Symposium.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcripts of interviews given by Coleman. B. 222, F. 14 contains materials originally found within a binder. F. 15 contains correspondence relating to an oral history project with the Bunche Center for African American Studies at UCLA, but does not contain transcript of said interview.

did.unittitle: PR: Posters, Flyers, Calendars, and Programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originals and printouts/photocopies. Highlights include flyers for various poetry readings at Al's Bar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items were originally in a binder. Highlights include course outline and student evaluations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Wanda Coleman held the appointment of Fletcher Jones Chair at LMU from 1994-1996, after which she was terminated. In addition to student work and course materials, evidence toward and correspondence regarding Coleman's desire to file a wrongful termination suit against the university is included.

did.unittitle: Loyola Marymount University (student essays on Coleman)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 217, folders 1-2 contain materials relating to Coleman's eviction from LMU campus housing (folder contents were removed from their original binder). Includes polaroids of the apartment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with students.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items from folders 4 and 5 were originally in a binder together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to Coleman's unemployment and job hunt, including applications and advertisements for various jobs. Folder 1 contains materials that were removed from a binder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notecards used by Coleman to keep track of various gigs. Each notecard contains a date and a description of the appearance or gig that occurred.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Over her life, Coleman created many notebooks and files of clippings from various newspapers and periodicals. Subseries also includes activist materials such as flyers and periodicals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Coleman's master list of clippings files by topic.

did.unittitle: Clippings: Health and Science-Media and Racism
Clippings: Media and Racism-Racial Incidents, Cultural Nationalism, Related Issues
Clippings: Racial Incidents, Cultural Nationalism, Related Issues
Clippings: Religion and Racism/The Occult/The Church
Clippings: Religion and Racism/The Occult/The Church-Statistics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings that have been cut out and taped to notecards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes a sample of her personal book collection and copies of her published books; periodicals which Coleman either collected or in which she was published; photos; and other materials such as awards, journals and diaries, administrative documents, and posthumous tributes.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books, publications, and AV material owned by Coleman.

Book titles in this collection have been individually cataloged and can be discovered in the Library catalog. Records for these items may be found by keyword searching this string in the catalog: "Coleman, Wanda, former owner."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Coleman's published works are listed first, as well as any materials related to her published works. Publications are listed in alphabetical order; if Coleman's work appears in a publication, it is listed in the scope and contents of the file. Publications that have been individually indexed are from the 2017 addition; boxes 178-179 contain an assortment of magazines from the original 2015 acquisition.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: THIS SUBSERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Individual copies of Coleman's entire bibliography, many of which were copies she used at poetry readings and have been signed or annotated by the author. Also contains anthologies featuring Coleman's work.

Titles in this collection have been individually cataloged and can be discovered in the Library catalog. Records for these items may be found by keyword searching this string in the catalog: "Coleman, Wanda, former owner."

did.unittitle: Art In The Court of the Blue Fag (chapbook), no. 59. Santa Barbara.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman's first published chapbook. Signed by the author. Chapbook published as part of "Sparrow," a pamphlet series published monthly by Black Sparrow Press. Issue no. 59.

did.unittitle: Wanda Coleman Greatest Hits: 1966-2003 (chapbook), no. 13. Columbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chapbook/serial published as part of Pudding House Publications "Poets Greatest Hits" series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple copies of each poem, all signed by author. "Blind Cassandra" printed by X-Ray Book Co., "April in Hollywood" printed by Pour Souls Press, "Who Raps for the Dead Lecturer" and "Moon Cherries," both printed by Sore Dove Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unfolded book covers for the following books by Coleman: Heavy Daughter Blues, African Sleeping Sickness, Bath Water Wine, Mambo Hips and Make Believe, Mercurochrome, Ostinato Vamps, and The Love Project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Small sample of Coleman's personal book collection. Box 181 contains photocopies and/or bound manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "The One Who Can" and "The Wait"

did.unittitle: The A.B.E.T. Call Sheet, no. 1. Beverly Hills.
African American Review, v. 45, no. 4. Baltimore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue is dedicated to Nelson Mandela and Wanda Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-published chapbook by Austin Straus. Inscribed to Coleman.

did.unittitle: Amerasia Journal, v. 19, no. 2. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of uncorrected proofs. Story: "The Arab Clerk". Includes correspondence.

did.unittitle: American Visions, v. 3, no. 6. Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "San Diego", "American Sonnet (2)", "The Secret Tongue of the Gone", and "Scylla and Charybdis"

did.unittitle: Black Film Review, v. 3, no. 4. and v. 4, no. 1-2. Washington, D.C.
Black Renaissance/Renaissance Noire, v. 10, no. 2-3. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "They Abandon the search for Federico","A Talk with my Grandson, Age Six", "Grus and Fuss", "Where My Blues Done Been", "What the Gin Rummy Queen Taught Me", and "One Man Acts Alone Against Death".

did.unittitle: The Black Scholar, v. 20, no. 2; v. 21, no. 1-2. Oakland.
The Blatant Image, no. 3. Sunny Valley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Picture of Coleman giving a reading by Linda Macaluso on page 43.

did.unittitle: Body and Soul, v. 1, no. 2. Dallas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "King of the Jews". Coleman is also listed as the Los Angeles editor for the magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "Salvation Wax" (excerpt)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed. Contains poem entitled "Mutant Angel Speaks" as well as an interview with editor Sid Miller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Reflections on a Yardbird" and "dancer in the window"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nonfiction: "Paying For Santa".

did.unittitle: Callaloo, v.22, no. 1; v. 26, no. 3. Baltimore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journals appear to have been restapled so that they only contain Coleman's pieces. 22.1 is signed by Coleman and features "Letters to E. Ethelbert Miller." 26.3 contains an interview with Coleman by Priscilla Ann Brown.

did.unittitle: Cape of Good Hope IMVO/News, no. 4. Pasadena.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special one-time issue entitled "Mary Jane: A Journal of Modern Cannibinology." Poem entitled "The Mountains/Entrails Curling Down and Out the Hole in the Side."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Featured on cover. Includes interview and selection of poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Beyond Baroque", "Death 213" and "dancer in the window".

did.unittitle: Ebony, v. XLI, no. 1; v. XLIII, no. 5. Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "Old Black Lady Next Door, Walking"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "when i sense..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features interview with Coleman. Also includes correspondence regarding interview.

did.unittitle: Essence, v. 1, no. 4, 7-9; v. 2, no. 1-2; v. 6, no. 1; and v. 11, no. 1. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features essays on Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Special issue guest edited by Coleman.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: File contains one compact disc inside magazine. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

did.unittitle: Index on Censorship, v. 17, no. 1. London.
Jack Foley Greatest Hits: 1974-2003 (chapbook), no. 210. Columbus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to Coleman.

did.unittitle: Journal of Black Poetry/Kitabu Cha Jua, v. 1, no. 11, 13, 15, 16, and 18, San Francisco.
L.A. Alternative Press, v. 2, no. 16. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper includes a feature story on Coleman.

did.unittitle: L.A. Jazz and Alternative Music, v. 2, no. 13. North Hollywood.
Los Angeles Free Press, v.9, no. 10-11; v. 10, no. 11, 17, 31, 38. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: V. 9, no. 10 features article "Angela's Big Night" by Coleman. V. 9, no. 11 features response from Angela Davis in regards to Coleman's article. V. 10, no. 10 features interview with Jerry Butler by Coleman. V. 10, no. 17 features article "The Crusaders Drop All That Jazz" by Coleman. V. 10, no. 31 features interview with Nikki Giovanni by Coleman. V.10, no. 38 features interview with Martha Reeves by Coleman.

did.unittitle: The Los Angeles Review, v. 6. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed. Issue is dedicated to Coleman. Includes feature on Coleman as well as two poems, "Hecuba on Sunset" and "I Love the Dark".

did.unittitle: The Los Angeles Times. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "Manuela Come Out of the Kitchen". Includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Multi-Ethnic Literature of the United States." Copy is inscribed to Coleman. Issue features interview with Coleman. Also contains correspondence and a CD of the radio play "Voodoo Lady" by Odie Hawkins, seemingly unrelated to journal.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Contains one CD. Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

did.unittitle: The Militant, v. 35, no. 31. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "The Hives", "Junk Mail Koans", "Magna Cum Laude, U of the Streets" and "Identifying Marks". Includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prose Poem: "Angel Baby Blues".

did.unittitle: New Perspectives Quarterly, v.5 , no. 3. Santa Barbara.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Story: "Ghana Ghana Ghanu".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue no. 4 contains Coleman's annotations. Volume 9 features a new editor, as Coleman left the magazine in 1974.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Noise", "Dicksboro Romance" and "The Sweetening".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman's reading is listed in the event calendar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features interview with Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "American Sonnet (36)".

did.unittitle: The Reggae and African Beat, v. 2, no. 7-8; v. 3, no. 1-2; 5-6; v. 13, no. 3. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Box 207 contains v. 13, no. 3, the 12th Annual Bob Marley and the Wailers collector's edition, which features a reprint of Coleman's interview with Marley, originally published in L.A. Free Press, 1973.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Talk About The Money" and "Strayhorn"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Los Angeles Born and Buried", "Voices", "Susannah Your Smallness and Cedar Hair" and "Simon"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Black Madonna", "Confessions Noir" and "Chuck Man"

did.unittitle: Scientific American, v. 259, no. 4. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features picture of Coleman.

did.unittitle: Southern California Lit Scene, v.1, no. 1. Long Beach.
TIME, v. 84, no. 5 and v. 86, no. 8. New York.
Together: The UCLA Feminist Magazine. Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Features article on Coleman.

did.unittitle: Turning The Tide, v.15, no. 1. Culver City.
The Village Voice: Jazz Special. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "but, ruby my dear" and "Cousin Mary".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems: "Negative Portions" and "Late Broadcast news".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Outlook" section only. Article: "But Blacks Have Been Left Behind".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poem: "Return to Skull Island".

did.unittitle: Z Miscellaneous, v.1, no. 3. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fiction: "Chuck and the Boss Man's Wife". Poem: "San Anselmo 1966".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and clippings of Coleman's writings as well as reviews and mentions about Coleman as featured in various publications.

list.item: Box 236: 4 VHS tapes, 1 betamax tape, 15 CDs and 1 DVD.
Box 237: 30 CDs and 32 floppy discs.
Box 238: 5 vinyl records.
Box 239: 28 audiocassette tapes.
Box 240: 31 audiocassette tapes.
Box 241: 4 DVDs, 3 CDs, and 3 audiocasette tapes.
Box 242: 29 audiocassette tapes and 1 digital audio tape.
Box 243: 5 CDs, 20 audiocassette tapes.

Box 236, VHS/Betmax tapes labeled: "Angel Street - Final Temp Dub 5-13-92"; "EDEN Theatrical Workshop Special 2-15-03"; "Amber Tamblyn: A Poetry Reading at Beyond Baroque" (with Coleman as special guest); "'The L.A. Disc' Raw Film-to-Tape Transfer"; and "McNeil-Lehrer report 11-24-87". Poetry compilations featuring Coleman including CDs "A Nation of Poets" (1989 live recording, National Black Arts Festival, 2 copies); "JazzSpeak: A Word Collection" (1991 New Alliance Records),"Black and Tan Club" (1991 New Alliance Records); "DisClosure: Voices of Women" (1992 New Alliance Records), "Our Souls Have Grown Deep Like the Rivers" (2000 Rhino Entertainment, 2 copies). Coleman's solo work includes CDs "Black Angeles: Michelle T. Clinton and Wanda Coleman" (1988 New Alliance Records); "High Priestess of Word (1990 New Alliance Records, 2 copies); Black and Blue News (1990 Widowspeak, 2 copies); and "Beserk on Hollywood Blvd" (1991 New Alliance Records, 2 copies). 1 DVD labeled "Beyond Baroque".

In box 237, CD and DVD labels include: "Crossroads Slamnation"; "The Wanda Coleman Project Trailer"; "Mississippi Fred McDowell Show"; "Mad Dog Black Lady"; "Beyond Baroque 6-16-12"; "Wanda Coleman interviewed by Jack Foley 2004"; "Paris 3-17-06"; "Wanda's Brochures 8-10-03"; "Photos of Dr. Watson"; "Me from Lydia Lunch Photo 6-99"; and "WarChild JTull". Includes copy of Coleman interviewed on "Northwestern Exposure" radio show (#320, 11/26/00) and "The Verdict and the Violence" (1992 Idiot Savant Records). Floppy discs contain Coleman's written work.

Box 238 includes 2 copies of "Twin Sisters," Coleman's spoken word album with Exene Cervenka and 1 copy of "Black and Blue News," a solo recording. Also includes "A Working Man Can't Get Nowhere Today" by Merle Haggard and the Strangers and "The Best of the Delfonics."

Box 239, known Poetry Connexion cassette tapes include: "Susanna Foster 12-19-81"; "#153 Dr. Mongo Tribubu, Drew Lesso 9-19-87"; "Ellen Maybe 7-17-93"; "Fundraiser 6-20-87"; "Will Margolis, Frank Rios 7-2-88"; "Carine Topal 10-31-87; "Jane Hirschfield 2-18-89"; "Charles Webb #49"; "Jerome Rothenberg 7-16"; "Prof. Talat Halmont 3-21-92"; and "Deena Metzer 2-1-93". Additional tapes may or may not be related to the Poetry Connexion; labels include: "Michael O'Sheel"; "Charles Bivins 5-31-6"; "Amy Uyematsu 7-31-93"; "Kyle Bates"; "Martha Lifson"; "Joanna Warwick"; "Steve Allen"; "Ruth Forman 8-7-93"; "Ben Saltman/Jodi Johnson 12-17-83"; "Donahue Interview"; "M. Pearloff"; "Roger Suva 5-4-5"; "James Regen 8-16-86"; "Jennifer Olds 11-30-5"; and "Bayla Winters 6-4-88" (includes correspondence.) Additional 2 tapes with Pacifica Radio Archive labels, titled "Daddy, Do I Dare to Eat a Peach" and "Nicolas Guillen".

Box 240 cassette labels: "Akilah Oliver, Ntozake Shange, Partow Nooriala - LA Festival 8-21-93"; "Students from Room 21 Teacher - Ms. Tomblin 2-9-87"; "Exile Shadows"; "7 min - Vitale - NP3 3/19"; "Jane, Jane"; "Me and Kalin at The Venice Jail - Liza Jane Braude Hostess 9-20-80"; "Sergio Zenteno 5-3-86"; "K Lincoln 4-19-6 (show #2)"; "Bill Mohr 10-4-85"; "Partow Nooriala and Shahrzhad Sepanlou 1/15/94"; "Jay Linthicum Interview 3-14-78 San Diego/Shirley Williams San Diego 3-16-78"; "Dit Lawrence on Cezanne pt. 2 9-17-94"; "Jazz Speak Dub"; "Morning Becomes Eclectic KCRW 8-30-91"; "Ruler of My Heart"; "Voices of the Angels - Produced by Harvey Kubernik/Freeway Records"; "Susan Anderson KPFK interview broadcast w/ Harvey Kubernik 7-20-85 'Twin Sisters'"; "Black and Tan"; "Suzanne Lummis KPFK Reading 6-4-83"; "Rehearsal Tape Sun 4/14/91"; "Reparations Show 8/13/03 Earl O Hutchinson"; "Earthling Bookstore Reading 11-2-83"; "Redondo Beach interview w/ Me and Lee Hickman"; "Bobbi Sykes Interview" (two tapes)"; "Textures Bukowski show"; and "Della" (4 tapes - most likely interview with Della Patton. See correspondence in Box 184, Folder 14 for more information).

Box 241 contains some AV materials with additional correspondence. DVD labels: "The Wanda Coleman Project" (2014, 2 copies); "Los Angeles Now" (2004 PBS); and "Land of Poets" (a compilation of SoCal poetry performances). CDs labels: "Mad Dog Black Lady"; "KCSN 88.5 Let's Do Lunch Wednesday 1/5/00" (interview with Wanda Coleman by Rene Engel); and "Beyond Baroque." Cassette tape labels: "Storylines California 11-21-99"; "The Art of Poetry: Identity and Voices" (copy of Coleman's performance at the Los Angeles Festival of Books); and "Blacks and Jews-Stereotypes."

Box 242 cassette labels: "On Theloniusism 10-15-89"; "Kate Pearcy Interview 1-12-95" (Australian interview of Coleman); "Wanda and Marv 4-8-79 Riverside"; "NAR 048 Wanda Coleman" (two tapes); "Me + Austin 9/5/81 Reading at Plum's Bookstore"; "Vinewood Reading + Stephen 5-4-79"; "Xene and Me"; "SDSU Reading 2/23/84"; "Charles McClain/Wanda Coleman 10-16-98"; "Voices of writers KBOO Radio 4/1/90"; "Reed College 1/15/90 and Talking Earth 1/3/90" (2 tapes); "Antler 5-21-94"; "Sonia Sanchez interviewed by Coleman and Nancy Schiffrin at the Los Angeles center following her 11-17-86 reading"; "LA Riots 10 Years Later" and "The Invisible Man? The New African American Experience" from the LA Times Festival of Books; "Inspiration House KPFK 3-27-00"; "Kate Gale, Austin Straus, Wanda Coleman recorded by Jack Foley"; "Expanding Reparations Panel 4-8-00" (two tapes); "Spoken Word Festival 98"; "Wanda Coleman Demo High Priestess"; "Huh (Trouble)"; "Berserk on Hollywood Blvd."; "High Priestess of Word"; "Hollyword" (compilation); "A Nation of Poets" (live recording from National Black Arts Festival 1990). Digital Audio Tape: "Spoken Word Festival Stockholm 98". Box 243 CD Labels: "Kurt Elling Nightmoves"; "Phil Ochs"; "My Names Not Rodriguez" (Luis Rodriguez, 2003 Dos Manos Records); "The Crumbling Empire of White People" (Mr. Smolin, 2007 Nomenclature Records); and "Old School Rap Party" (2002 Thump Records). Audiocassette labels: "Dan Kwong 8-15-92"; "Gary Snyder Live 3/4/85"; "Richard J. Weekley 1986"; "Mirror Images - The Poetry of Toby Lurie"; "Panic * DJ - Bob Holman"; "Michael C. Ford"; "The More I Drink The Better You Look - F.A. Nettelbeck"; "Power of the Dreamer - Jimmy Townes - The Trance Band"; "Willie Sims - Good Talk"; "Willie Sims - Hot Stuff"; "Rhythm Activism"; "Tender Hooks - Tina Fulker"; "Sketches - Dr. Carl A. Faber"; "Blackrobe: Isaac Jogues - Poems by Maurice Kenny"; "Dr. Mongo Taribubu/Drew Lesso"; "blackmadrid - Keith Antar Mason"; "the friendly people, for JESUS"; "Poeticus Umbilicus - Beata Pozniak"; "Saturday Dancer - Aida Pavletich - Norma Tanega" (signed to Coleman); and "Jimi Hendrix: Interviews 1967-1970".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Early writings of Coleman's dating from 1961-1971 and a few more recent works of visual art by Coleman. Includes school notebooks containing class assignments, sketches and drawings; a small collection of periodicals, and early stories, poems, and essays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Coleman's piece "Emotional Blackmail," submitted to the Whitney Ray Johnson Exhibition, and some drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coleman often returned to her journals, so entries span different different periods of time. Many journals and notebooks also contain correspondence that has been tucked between pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed pages of diary entries while Coleman was on a speaking tour of Australia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boxes contain a mix of personal and professional photos, in the form of prints, polaroids, slides, and negatives. Photographs are both loose as well as collected within scrapbooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Box 183 includes plaques and awards. Box 234 contains certificates for various awards including Coleman's Emmy. Folder 5 contains programs for award ceremonies honoring Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph book from Coleman's elementary school. Includes additional page tucked in with a signature from Phil Ochs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Olympia Traveller de Luxe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder 1 contains journals belonging to Coleman's son, Tony, written when he was diagnosed as HIV positive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents include birth, death, and marriage certificates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to the Los Angeles poetry and art scene/community.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains calling card caddy (business card holder), blank postcards, and a name tag from the Los Angeles Times Festival of Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to Compton Community College, including a program for the 7th Annual African People's Conference, a flyer for a May Day Bazaar and Swapmeet hosted by the Nation of Islam/Respect For Life Organization, and a copy of The Final Call, the National of Islam newspaper (V. 7, no. 10).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Knick knacks and items from the top of Coleman's office bookcase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to or created after Coleman's death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyers and pamphlets for various tribute events in celebration of Coleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed guest book from Coleman's memorial service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents relating to the appraisal of Coleman's literary archive, before purchase by UCLA. Also contains UCLA purchase agreement with Austin Straus and documents relating to both a fire and flood in the storage unit where Coleman's papers were kept before purchase.
